news.title: Restaurant Bouchon du Centre stopt na 27 jaar: ‘De mensen ga ik missen, maar Amsterdam niet’ - Het Parool
news.summary:
# Summary

Bouchon du Centre, a French restaurant in Amsterdam that operated for 27 years, has permanently closed. The owner expressed that while they will miss the clientele, they won't miss Amsterdam itself. Visitors to Amsterdam looking for established French dining options will need to find alternatives, as this long-standing neighborhood institution is no longer operating.

news.title: Newcastle disease reappears in Germany for first time since 1966 - foodagribusiness.world
news.summary:
# Summary

This article reports on Newcastle disease, a poultry virus, resurfacing in Germany after nearly 60 years of absence. While the article doesn't mention a specific restaurant or food establishment, this news is relevant to food safety and diners in Germany, as the outbreak could potentially affect poultry supply chains, restaurant menus, and food availability in the region. Visitors to Germany should be aware that local poultry restrictions or shortages may temporarily impact dining options featuring chicken and other bird-based dishes.
